headline: Court of Appeal: no court can review an emergency call — issuance or refusal
context: On 4 May 2026 the Court of Appeal dismissed lawyer Syed Iskandar Syed Jaafar's challenge to the Agong's October 2020 refusal of PM Muhyiddin's emergency request. The panel held Article 150(8) — inserted by Act A514 in 1981 — ousts review of both issuance and refusal.

cards.big: Court of Appeal: the Agong's emergency call — issue it, or refuse it — is final. No court may ask why.
cards.sub: The challenge was to a refusal (October 2020). The same architecture covers issuance — under which Parliament did not sit for seven months in 2021.

cards.big: Article 150(8): the Agong's satisfaction 'shall be final and conclusive and shall not be challenged or called in question in any court on any ground.'
cards.sub: Parliament added 150(8) and (9) by Act A514 in May 1981, to oust review of emergencies after the Privy Council's 1979 Teh Cheng Poh ruling.

cards.big: In 2018 the Federal Court ruled judicial review is part of the Constitution's basic structure — and ouster clauses cannot remove it (Indira Gandhi).
cards.sub: The 4 May panel accepted the doctrine — but said emergency is policy-centric (security, foreign relations, public order) and outside court competence.

cards.big: Five federal emergencies since 1964 (1964, 1966, 1969, 1977, 2021). One documented refusal on record: October 2020.
cards.sub: That refusal came after the Agong met seven Malay Rulers at Istana Negara on 25 October 2020. With courts now out, it is the only documented refusal the system has produced.

cards.big: Two checks survive in text: Parliament's Art. 150(3) annulment, and the Conference of Rulers. In 2021, Parliament did not sit while the emergency ran.
Like a referee whose call and non-call both stand — with no replay rule on either.
cards.sub: The Agong issued the 2021 emergency on Cabinet advice. He refused the 2020 request despite Cabinet advice. The 4 May ruling closes the courtroom on both.

cards.big: 1979 Privy Council: reviewable. 1981 Parliament: ousted. 2018 Federal Court: ouster invalid. 2026 Court of Appeal: emergencies excluded. Only political checks remain.
